contentBlocks.original:
अयि गिरिनंदिनि नंदितमेदिनि विश्वविनोदिनि नंदनुते
गिरिवरविंध्यशिरोधिनिवासिनि विष्णुविलासिनि जिष्णुनुते ।
भगवति हे शितिकण्ठकुटुंबिनि भूरिकुटुंबिनि भूरिकृते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi girinandini nanditamedini viśvavinodini naṃdanute
girivaraviṃdhyaśirodhinivāsini viṣṇuvilāsini jiṣṇunute .
Bhagavati he śitikaṇṭhakuṭuṃbini bhūrikuṭuṃbini bhūrikṛte
jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 1..
contentBlocks.translation:
1. Mother Durga – The Daughter of the Mountain and Joy of the World
O daughter of the mountain, who makes the whole earth happy, who makes the whole universe rejoice, praised by Nandin. Dwelling on the peak of the great Vindhya mountain, glittering widely, (variation? giver of joy to Vishnu), praised by those desirous of victory. O Goddess, wife of the blue necked Siva, One who has many families, One who has done a lot, be victorious, be victorious, O destroyer of the demon mahisa, with beautiful braids of hair, daughter of the mountain Himalaya.
contentBlocks.meanings: अयि (ayi) – O; गिरिनंदिनि (girinandini) – daughter of the mountain (giri:mountain | nandini:daughter); नंदितमेदिनि (nanditamedini) – who makes the earth happy (nandita:made happy | medini:earth); विश्वविनोदिनि (viśvavinodini) – who makes the universe rejoice (viśva:universe | vinodini:who makes rejoice); नंदनुते (nandanute) – praised by Nandin (nanda:Nandin | nute:praised by); गिरिवरविंध्यशिरोधिनिवासिनि (girivaraviṃdhyaśirodhinivāsini) – dwelling on Vindhya mountain peak (girivara:great mountain | viṃdhya:Vindhya | śirodhi:peak | nivāsini:dwelling); विष्णुविलासिनि (viṣṇuvilāsini) – giver of joy to Vishnu (viṣṇu:Vishnu | vilāsini:giver of joy); जिष्णुनुते (jiṣṇunute) – praised by the victorious (jiṣṇu:victorious | nute:praised by); भगवति (bhagavati) – O Goddess; हे (he) – O; शितिकण्ठकुटुंबिनि (śitikaṇṭhakuṭuṃbini) – wife of blue-necked Siva (śitikaṇṭha:blue-necked | kuṭuṃbini:wife); भूरिकुटुंबिनि (bhūrikuṭuṃbini) – one with many families (bhūri:many | kuṭuṃbini:families); भूरिकृते (bhūrikṛte) – one who has done a lot (bhūri:a lot | kṛte:done); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – destroyer of demon Mahisa (mahiṣa:Mahisa | asura:demon | mardini:destroyer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – with beautiful braids (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braids); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
सुरवरवर्षिणि दुर्धरधर्षिणि दुर्मुखमर्षिणि हर्षरते
त्रिभुवनपोषिणि शंकरतोषिणि किल्बिषमोषिणि घोषरते ।
दनुजनिरोषिणि दितिसुतरोषिणि दुर्मदशोषिणि सिन्धुसुते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Suravaravarṣiṇi durdharadharṣiṇi durmukhamarṣiṇi harṣarate
tribhuvanapoṣiṇi śaṃkaratoṣiṇi kilbiṣamoṣiṇi ghoṣarate .
Danujaniroṣiṇi ditisutaroṣiṇi durmadaśoṣiṇi sindhusute
jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 2..
contentBlocks.translation:
2. Mother Durga – Destroys the Danavas and the Daityas and Nourishes the Three Worlds
O bestower of boons on Gods, One who assails those hard to control, who tolerates those with ugly faces (?), one engrossed in rejoicing . One who nourishes the three worlds, One who pleases sankara, One who removes sins, One who engrosses in sound of Om (?) . One who is angry with the progeny of Danu (demon), One who is angry with the sons of Diti (also demon), One who destroys those with evil intoxication of pride, daughter of the ocean.
contentBlocks.meanings: सुरवरवर्षिणि (suravaravarṣiṇi) – bestower of boons on gods (sura:god | vara:boon | varṣiṇi:bestower); दुर्धरधर्षिणि (durdharadharṣiṇi) – assails those hard to control (durdhara:hard to control | dharṣiṇi:assails); दुर्मुखमर्षिणि (durmukhamarṣiṇi) – tolerates those with ugly faces (durmukha:ugly faces | marṣiṇi:tolerates); हर्षरते (harṣarate) – engrossed in rejoicing (harṣa:rejoicing | rate:engrossed in); त्रिभुवनपोषिणि (tribhuvanapoṣiṇi) – nourishes the three worlds (tri:three | bhuvana:worlds | poṣiṇi:nourishes); शंकरतोषिणि (śaṃkaratoṣiṇi) – pleases Śaṅkara (śaṃkara:Śaṅkara | toṣiṇi:pleases); किल्बिषमोषिणि (kilbiṣamoṣiṇi) – removes sins (kilbiṣa:sins | moṣiṇi:removes); घोषरते (ghoṣarate) – engrossed in sound (ghoṣa:sound | rate:engrossed in); दनुजनिरोषिणि (danujaniroṣiṇi) – angry with progeny of Danu (danuja:progeny of Danu | niroṣiṇi:angry with); दितिसुतरोषिणि (ditisutaroṣiṇi) – angry with sons of Diti (diti:Diti | suta:sons | roṣiṇi:angry with); दुर्मदशोषिणि (durmadaśoṣiṇi) – destroys evil intoxication of pride (durmada:evil intoxication of pride | śoṣiṇi:destroys); सिन्धुसुते (sindhusute) – daughter of the ocean (sindhu:ocean | sute:daughter); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahiṣāsura (mahiṣāsura:Mahiṣāsura | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
अयि जगदंब मदंब कदंबवनप्रियवासिनि हासरते
शिखरिशिरोमणि तुङ्गहिमालय श‍ृंगनिजालय मध्यगते ।
मधुमधुरे मधुकैटभगंजिनि कैटभभंजिनि रासरते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi jagadaṃba madaṃba kadaṃbavanapriyavāsini hāsarate
śikhariśiromaṇituṅgahimālayaśṛṃganijālayamadhyagate .
Madhumadhure madhukaiṭabhagaṃjini kaiṭabhabhaṃjini rāsarate
jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 3..
contentBlocks.translation:
3. Mother Durga – Destroyer of the Demons Madhu and Kaitabha
O mother of the world, my mother, One who loves to dwell in a forest of Kadamba trees, One who keeps on smiling . One who is on her own dwelling on the tall peak of the Himalaya, the greatest among the mountains . One who is very sweet like honey, One who has the treasure of demons Madhu and Kaitabha, destroyer of the demon Kaitabha, engaged in dancing.
contentBlocks.meanings: अयि (ayi) – O; जगदंब (jagadamba) – mother of the world (jagat:world | amba:mother); मदंब (madamba) – my mother (mad:my | amba:mother); कदंबवनप्रियवासिनि (kadaṃbavanapriyavāsini) – one who loves Kadamba forest (kadaṃba:Kadamba | vana:forest | priya:beloved | vāsini:dweller); हासरते (hāsarate) – engaged in smiling; शिखरिशिरोमणि (śikhariśiromaṇi) – crest jewel of peaks (śikhari:peak | śiraḥ:head | maṇi:jewel); तुङ्गहिमालय (tuṅgahimālaya) – tall Himalaya (tuṅga:tall | himālaya:Himalaya); श‍ृंगनिजालय (śṛṃganijālaya) – dwelling on the peak (śṛṃga:peak | nija:own | ālaya:dwelling); मध्यगते (madhyagate) – situated in the middle; मधुमधुरे (madhumadhure) – sweet like honey (madhu:honey | madhura:sweet); मधुकैटभगंजिनि (madhukaiṭabhagaṃjini) – destroyer of Madhu and Kaitabha (madhu:Madhu | kaiṭabha:Kaitabha | gaṃjini:destroyer); कैटभभंजिनि (kaiṭabhabhaṃjini) – destroyer of Kaitabha (kaiṭabha:Kaitabha | bhaṃjini:destroyer); रासरते (rāsarate) – engaged in dancing; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – destroyer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:destroyer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
अयि शतखण्ड विखण्डितरुण्ड वितुण्डितशुण्द गजाधिपते
रिपुगजगण्ड विदारणचण्ड पराक्रमशुण्ड मृगाधिपते ।
निजभुजदण्ड निपातितखण्ड विपातितमुण्ड भटाधिपते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi śatakhaṇḍa vikhaṇḍitaruṇḍa vituṇḍitaśuṇda gajādhipate
Ripugajagaṇḍa vidāraṇacaṇḍa parākramaśuṇḍa mṛgādhipate .
Nijabhujadaṇḍa nipātitakhaṇḍa vipātitamuṇḍa bhaṭādhipate
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 4 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
4. Mother Durga – Destroyer of the Demons Chanda and Munda
O One who split the heads (of demons) into hundreds of pieces and One who cut the trunks of great battle elephants . whose great lion is skilled in terrifying valor in tearing apart the temples of enemy elephants . One who has cut down into pieces the heads of enemy chieftains with the strength of her own arms.
contentBlocks.meanings: अयि (ayi) – O; शतखण्ड (śatakhaṇḍa) – hundred pieces (śata:hundred | khaṇḍa:pieces); विखण्डितरुण्ड (vikhaṇḍitaruṇḍa) – split heads (vikhaṇḍita:split | ruṇḍa:heads); वितुण्डितशुण्ड (vituṇḍitaśuṇḍa) – cut trunks (vituṇḍita:cut | śuṇḍa:trunks); गजाधिपते (gajādhipate) – O lord of elephants (gaja:elephant | adhipate:O lord); रिपुगजगण्ड (ripugajagaṇḍa) – enemy elephant temples (ripu:enemy | gaja:elephant | gaṇḍa:temples); विदारणचण्ड (vidāraṇacaṇḍa) – terrifying valor (vidāraṇa:tearing apart | caṇḍa:terrifying); पराक्रमशुण्ड (parākramaśuṇḍa) – valor trunk (parākrama:valor | śuṇḍa:trunk); मृगाधिपते (mṛgādhipate) – O lord of beasts (mṛga:beast | adhipate:O lord); निजभुजदण्ड (nijabhujadaṇḍa) – own arm strength (nija:own | bhuja:arm | daṇḍa:strength); निपातितखण्ड (nipātitakhaṇḍa) – cut into pieces (nipātita:cut | khaṇḍa:pieces); विपातितमुण्ड (vipātitamuṇḍa) – severed heads (vipātita:severed | muṇḍa:heads); भटाधिपते (bhaṭādhipate) – O lord of warriors (bhaṭa:warrior | adhipate:O lord); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – destroyer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:destroyer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
अयि रणदुर्मद शत्रुवधोदित दुर्धरनिर्जर शक्तिभृते
चतुरविचार धुरीणमहाशिव दूतकृत प्रमथाधिपते ।
दुरितदुरीह दुराशयदुर्मति दानवदुत कृतान्तमते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi raṇadurmada śatruvadhodita durdharanirjara śaktibhṛte
Caturavicāra dhurīṇamahāśiva dūtakṛta pramathādhipate .
Duritadurīha durāśayadurmati dānavaduta kṛtāntamate
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 5 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
5. Mother Durga – Made Lord Shiva the Messenger against enemies.
O One who holds the invincible and undiminishing striking force which arose on the occasion of killing the enemies who were hard to subdue on the battlefield . who made Pramatha, the great attendant of Shiva, a leader in subtle thinking, her commander (?) . who decided to destroy the messenger of demons who were sinful, with evil intentions, thoughts and mind.
contentBlocks.meanings: अयि (ayi) – O; रणदुर्मद (raṇadurmada) – battle arrogance (raṇa:battle | durmada:arrogance); शत्रुवधोदित (śatruvadhodita) – arisen for enemy's killing (śatru:enemy | vadha:killing | udita:arisen); दुर्धरनिर्जर (durdharanirjara) – invincible and undiminishing (durdhara:invincible | nirjara:undiminishing); शक्तिभृते (śaktibhṛte) – holder of power (śakti:power | bhṛte:holder); चतुरविचार (caturavicāra) – subtle thinking (catura:subtle | vicāra:thinking); धुरीणमहाशिव (dhurīṇamahāśiva) – great attendant of Shiva (dhurīṇa:attendant | mahāśiva:great Shiva); दूतकृत (dūtakṛta) – made messenger (dūta:messenger | kṛta:made); प्रमथाधिपते (pramathādhipate) – lord of Pramatha (pramatha:Pramatha | adhipate:lord); दुरितदुरीह (duritadurīha) – sinful intentions (durita:sinful | durīha:intentions); दुराशयदुर्मति (durāśayadurmati) – evil thoughts and mind (durāśaya:evil thoughts | durmati:evil mind); दानवदुत (dānavaduta) – demon's messenger (dānava:demon | duta:messenger); कृतान्तमते (kṛtāntamate) – decided to destroy (kṛtānta:destroy | mate:decided); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣāsura:Mahishasura | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
अयि शरणागत वैरिवधुवर वीरवराभय दायकरे
त्रिभुवनमस्तक शुलविरोधि शिरोऽधिकृतामल शुलकरे ।
दुमिदुमितामर धुन्दुभिनादमहोमुखरीकृत दिङ्मकरे
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi śaraṇāgata vairivadhuvara vīravarābhaya dāyakare
Tribhuvanamastaka śulavirodhi śiro'dhikṛtāmala śulakare .
Dumidumitāmara dhundubhinādamahomukharīkṛta diṅmakare
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 6 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
6. Mother Durga – Pardons the Enemy Heroes when their Wives take Refuge
O One who gives protection to the great heroic husbands of the enemy wives who have come seeking refuge . One who holds in her hands a spotless spear pointed towards the head of the opponent who is causing a great pain for all the three worlds . One who is like the blazing hot sun, aroused by the power of resounding noise of the drums of Gods.
contentBlocks.meanings: अयि (ayi) – O; शरणागत (śaraṇāgata) – seeking refuge; वैरिवधुवर (vairivadhuvara) – enemy wives' great husbands (vaira:enemy | vadhu:wives | vara:great husbands); वीरवराभय (vīravarābhaya) – protection to great heroes (vīra:heroes | vara:great | abhaya:protection); दायकरे (dāyakare) – O giver; त्रिभुवनमस्तक (tribhuvanamastaka) – heads of the three worlds (tri:three | bhuvana:worlds | mastaka:heads); शुलविरोधि (śulavirodhi) – opponent of the spear (śula:spear | virodhi:opponent); शिरोऽधिकृत (śiro'dhikṛta) – head of the opponent (śiraḥ:head | adhikṛta:of the opponent); अमल (amala) – spotless; शुलकरे (śulakare) – holder of the spear (śula:spear | kare:holder); दुमिदुमितामर (dumidumitāmara) – resounding noise of the gods (dumi:resounding | dumita:noise | amara:gods); धुन्दुभिनादमहोमुखरीकृत (dhundubhinādamahomukharīkṛta) – resounding with drum noise (dhundubhi:drum | nāda:noise | maha:great | mukhara:resounding | īkṛta:made); दिङ्मकरे (diṅmakare) – sun of directions (diṅ:directions | makara:sun); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahiṣāsura (mahiṣa:buffalo | asura:demon | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
अयि निजहुङ्कृति मात्रनिराकृत धूम्रविलोचन धूम्रशते
समरविशोषित शोणितबीज समुद्भवशोणित बीजलते ।
शिवशिवशुम्भ निशुम्भमहाहव तर्पितभूत पिशाचरते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi nijahum̐kṛti mātranirākṛta dhūmravilocana dhūmraśate
Samaraviśoṣita śoṇitabīja samudbhava śoṇitabījalate .
Śivaśiva śuṃbha niśuṃbha mahāhava tarpitabhūtapiśācarate
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 7..
contentBlocks.translation:
7. Mother Durga – Destroyer of the Demons Dhumralocana, Raktabija and Shumbha Nishumbha
O One who has blown aside hundreds of streams of smoke coming from demons with smoking eyes merely with her own roaring . who is like a vine of blood-drops grown from the dried blood drops in battle . One who delights in the company of auspicious Shiva, Shumbha, Nishumbha, and the spirits who were fed during the great battle.

8. Mother Durga – Destroys the Chaturanga of the Enemy
One who decks herself with dancing ornaments on throbbing limbs at the moment of the battle, making her bow ready . who killed the huge enemy soldiers with a shining sword and with (arrows from) a quiver which has golden brown spots . who made the battleground with fourfold army into a stage with a colorful drama with screaming little soldiers.

Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 9 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
9. Mother Durga – Whose Battle expresses as Celestial Dance
O you who take delight in the dancing of heavenly damsels made excellent by the acting out of tatatheyi-tatheyi-tatha/tathA! O you who are full of laughter, amorous gestures and hulAsa (meaning uncertain, but probably something along the lines of abandon)! O you who possess immeasurable love for afflicted persons bowing before you (i.e., seeking refuge in you)! O you who rejoice in the sustained/deep sound, dhimikaTa-dhikkaTa-dhikaTadhimi, of the mRdaGga drum! O vanquisher of the demon MahiSa! O one bearing attractive knotted hair! O daughter of the mountain! Victory to you!

10. Mother Durga – Occupies Half of the Body of Lord Shiva
Be victorious! be victorious! whose victory should be sung, praised by the whole universe ready to sing the praise extolling her victory . who attracted the attention of shiva by twinkling of bells making various sounds of dancing . who delights in beautiful singing and in dance-drama presented by a leading dancer acting out the role of an actress with half of his body.

O Divine Mother, I invoke You and take refuge in Your Auspicious Feet) Salutations to You O Divine Mother; I Invoke You; Whose Beautiful Mind is United with a Charming Appearance, (I Invoke You) Whose Beautiful Face makes Subserviant the Beauty of the Moon Light of Night by Hiding them with Its Own Beauty, Whose Beautiful Eyes Conquer the Beauty of the Bees by Its Own Beauty, Victory to You, Victory to You, (I take Refuge in Your Auspicious Feet) O the Destroyer of Demon Mahishasura; (Victory to You) Who Shine with Beautiful Locks of Hair and Who is the Daughter of the Mountain

Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 12 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
12. Mother Durga – Accompanied by Army of Jasmine-Like Girls against excellent Fighters
Salutations to the Divine Mother who is accompanied in the great battle against excellent wrestlers (fighters), by girls who appear tender like jasmine who are fighting against the enemies. whose accompaniments are composed of girls from the bheel tribe who are tender like creepers of village jasmine and buzz like swarms of bees (or crickets). On whose face plays a smile created By joy which appears like dawn shining forth with red colour and blossoming the excellent buds. Victory to you, the destroyer of the demon Mahishasura, who has beautiful locks of hair and who is the daughter of the mountain.

13. Mother Durga – From Whom Emerges all Arts, Beauty and Power
Who is in charge of huge royal elephants in fury whose rut is streaming down their temples incessantly . princess, the daughter of the ocean, who has the beauty of the moon, the ornament of all the three worlds . princess of cupid who enchants the minds desirous of ladies with beautiful teeth.

contentBlocks.translation:
14. Mother Durga – Whose Forehead is Stainless and Pure like a Lotus Petal
Whose spotless forehead is enhanced by the beautiful complexion, pure and delicate like that of lotus petals . whose flock of swans is moving sportingly with steps which are the marks of all beautiful arts . whose bees from the bakula trees meet on the tops of lotus flowers which are crowded with (their own) bees.
contentBlocks.meanings: कमलदलामल (kamaladalāmala) – pure like lotus petal (kamala:lotus | dala:petal | āmala:pure); कोमलकान्ति (komalakānti) – delicate beauty (komala:delicate | kānti:beauty); कलाकलितामल (kalākalitāmala) – enhanced by arts (kalā:arts | kalita:enhanced | āmala:pure); भाललते (bhālalate) – forehead creeper (bhāla:forehead | late:creeper); सकलविलास (sakalavilāsa) – all beautiful arts (sakala:all | vilāsa:beautiful arts); कलानिलयक्रम (kalānilayakrama) – abode of arts steps (kalā:arts | nilaya:abode | krama:steps); केलिचलत्कल (kelicalatkala) – sporting moving arts (keli:sporting | calat:moving | kala:arts); हंसकुले (haṃsakule) – in the flock of swans (haṃsa:swan | kule:in the flock); अलिकुलसङ्कुल (alikulasaṅkula) – crowded with bees (ali:bee | kula:group | saṅkula:crowded); कुवलयमण्डल (kuvalayamaṇḍala) – circle of lotuses (kuvalaya:lotus | maṇḍala:circle); मौलिमिलद्बकुलालिकुले (maulimiladbakulālikule) – crowned with bakula bees (mauli:crown | milad:meeting | bakula:bakula | ali:bee | kule:in the group); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:buffalo | asura:demon | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
करमुरलीरव वीजितकूजित लज्जितकोकिल मञ्जुमते
मिलितपुलिन्द मनोहरगुञ्जित रञ्जितशैल निकुञ्जगते ।
निजगणभूत महाशबरीगण सद्गुणसम्भृत केलितले
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Karamuralīrava vījitakūjita lajjitakokila mañjumate
Militapulinda manoharaguñjita rañjitaśaila nikuñjagate .
Nijagaṇabhūta mahāśabarīgaṇa sadguṇasambhṛta kelitale
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 15 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
15. Mother Durga – Whose Voice is Sweeter than Flute and Cuckoo
Whose sweet cooing sounds made with the flute held in her own hands have put to shame the Kokila bird and who has sweet thoughts . who is in colorful mountain groves pleasantly resounding with the assembled mountain folks . whose playbround is filled with good qualities of the flocks of the great tribal women who are manifestations of her own qualities.
contentBlocks.meanings: करमुरलीरव (karamuralīrava) – flute sound (kara:hand | muralī:flute | rava:sound); वीजितकूजित (vījitakūjita) – surpassing cooing (vījita:surpassing | kūjita:cooing); लज्जितकोकिल (lajjitakokila) – ashamed cuckoo (lajjita:ashamed | kokila:cuckoo); मञ्जुमते (mañjumate) – sweet-minded (mañju:sweet | mate:minded); मिलितपुलिन्द (militapulinda) – assembled tribal women (milita:assembled | pulinda:tribal women); मनोहरगुञ्जित (manoharaguñjita) – charming humming (manohara:charming | guñjita:humming); रञ्जितशैल (rañjitaśaila) – colorful mountain (rañjita:colorful | śaila:mountain); निकुञ्जगते (nikuñjagate) – in the groves (nikuñja:groves | gate:in); निजगणभूत (nijagaṇabhūta) – own group beings (nija:own | gaṇa:group | bhūta:beings); महाशबरीगण (mahāśabarīgaṇa) – great tribal women group (mahā:great | śabarī:tribal women | gaṇa:group); सद्गुणसम्भृत (sadguṇasambhṛta) – filled with good qualities (sadguṇa:good qualities | sambhṛta:filled with); केलितले (kelitale) – in the playground (keli:play | tale:in the place); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
कटितटपीत दुकूलविचित्र मयुखतिरस्कृत चन्द्ररुचे
प्रणतसुरासुर मौलिमणिस्फुर दंशुलसन्नख चन्द्ररुचे
जितकनकाचल मौलिमदोर्जित निर्भरकुञ्जर कुम्भकुचे
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Kaṭitaṭapīta dukūlavicitra mayukhatiraskṛta candraruce
Praṇatasurāsura maulimaṇisphura daṃśulasannakha candraruce
Jitakanakācala maulimadorjita nirbharakuñjara kumbhakuce
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 16 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
16. Mother Durga – On Whose Radiant Toe-Nails Prostate the Devas and the Asuras
Who has set aside the brilliance of the moon with the colorful rays coming from the yellow silk she is wearing on her waist . whose toe-nails shine like the moon because of the rays emanating form the crest jewels of the bowing gods and demons . whose breasts outshine the temples of wild elephants and the high peaks of the golden mountains.
contentBlocks.meanings: कटितटपीत (kaṭitaṭapīta) – yellow silk on waist (kaṭi:waist | taṭa:border | pīta:yellow); दुकूलविचित्र (dukūlavicitra) – colorful silk (dukūla:silk | vicitra:colorful); मयुखतिरस्कृत (mayukhatiraskṛta) – outshone by rays (mayūkha:rays | tiraskṛta:outshone); चन्द्ररुचे (candraruce) – moon-like radiance (candra:moon | ruci:radiance); प्रणतसुरासुर (praṇatasurāsura) – bowing gods and demons (praṇata:bowing | sura:god | asura:demon); मौलिमणिस्फुर (maulimaṇisphura) – crest jewels shining (mauli:crest | maṇi:jewel | sphura:shining); दंशुलसन्नख (daṃśulasannakha) – toe-nails shining (daṃśula:toe | sanna:shining | nakha:nail); चन्द्ररुचे (candraruce) – moon-like radiance (candra:moon | ruci:radiance); जितकनकाचल (jitakanakācala) – surpassing golden mountains (jita:surpassing | kanaka:golden | acala:mountain); मौलिमदोर्जित (maulimadorjita) – crest pride powerful (mauli:crest | mada:pride | urjita:powerful); निर्भरकुञ्जर (nirbharakuñjara) – abundant elephant (nirbhara:abundant | kuñjara:elephant); कुम्भकुचे (kumbhakuce) – pot-like breasts (kumbha:pot | kuca:breast); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
विजितसहस्रकरैक सहस्रकरैक सहस्रकरैकनुते
कृतसुरतारक सङ्गरतारक सङ्गरतारक सूनुसुते ।
सुरथसमाधि समानसमाधि समाधिसमाधि सुजातरते ।
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Vijitasahasrakaraika sahasrakaraika sahasrakaraikanute
Kṛtasuratāraka saṅgaratāraka saṅgaratāraka sūnusute .
Surathasamādhi samānasamādhi samādhisamādhi sujātarate .
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 17 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
17. Mother Durga – Who is Pleased with Devotional Contemplation of both: like Suratha as well as Samadhi
Oh Goddess, the only time praised by the thousand hands of Sahasrarjuna who had excelled the thousand-rayed Sun by his valour, having a son like Karttika who had fought a victorious battle in the clash between gods and Tarakasura, one for whom pleasure is created in the accomplishment (accorded by Şage Medhas) by giving justification for the self-same mental agony of (King) Suratha and (the vaisya) Samadhi, who has crushed the demon. Mahisha, wearing a charming knot of hair, Oh Daughter of Mountain (Himalaya), Victory to you, Victory to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: विजितसहस्रकरैक (vijitasahasrakarāika) – conqueror of thousand-handed (vijita:conqueror | sahasra:thousand | kara:handed | eka:one); सहस्रकरैक (sahasrakarāika) – thousand-handed (sahasra:thousand | kara:handed | eka:one); सहस्रकरैकनुते (sahasrakarāikanute) – praised by thousand-handed (sahasra:thousand | kara:handed | eka:one | nute:praised); कृतसुरतारक (kṛtasuratāraka) – accomplished Suratha's task (kṛta:accomplished | suratha:Suratha | āraka:task); सङ्गरतारक (saṅgaratāraka) – battle task (saṅgara:battle | tāraka:task); सङ्गरतारक (saṅgaratāraka) – battle task (saṅgara:battle | tāraka:task); सूनुसुते (sūnusute) – son's mother (sūnu:son | sute:mother); सुरथसमाधि (surathasamādhi) – Suratha's meditation (suratha:Suratha | samādhi:meditation); समानसमाधि (samānasamādhi) – equal meditation (samāna:equal | samādhi:meditation); समाधिसमाधि (samādhisamādhi) – meditation on meditation (samādhi:meditation | samādhi:meditation); सुजातरते (sujātarate) – well-born delight (su:well | jāta:born | rate:delight); जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – charming-haired (ramya:charming | kapardini:haired); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
पदकमलं करुणानिलये वरिवस्यति योऽनुदिनं सुशिवे
अयि कमले कमलानिलये कमलानिलयः स कथं न भवेत् ।
तव पदमेव परम्पदमित्यनुशीलयतो मम किं न शिवे
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Padakamalaṃ karuṇānilaye varivasyati yo'nudinaṃ suśive
Ayi kamale kamalānilaye kamalānilayaḥ sa kathaṃ na bhavet .
Tava padameva parampadamityanuśīlayato mama kiṃ na śive
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 18 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
18. Mother Durga – An Abode of Devi Mahalakshmi
O benevolent goddess accompanied by Shiva, if someone daily cherishes your lotuslike feet . (contd. from prev line) then, O lotus dwelling LakShmi, how will he not become wealthy? . O auspicious Goddess, is there anything that I would not have, if I earnestly believe that your feet are the highest goal to be achieved?
contentBlocks.meanings: पदकमलं (padakamalaṃ) – lotus-like feet (pada:feet | kamalaṃ:lotus-like); करुणानिलये (karuṇānilaye) – abode of compassion (karuṇā:compassion | nilaye:abode); वरिवस्यति (varivasyati) – cherishes; यः (yaḥ) – who; अनुदिनं (anudinaṃ) – daily; सुशिवे (suśive) – O benevolent goddess (su:good | śive:goddess); अयि (ayi) – O; कमले (kamale) – O lotus; कमलानिलये (kamalānilaye) – abode of Lakshmi (kamalā:Lakshmi | nilaye:abode); कमलानिलयः (kamalānilayaḥ) – lotus dwelling (kamalā:lotus | nilayaḥ:dwelling); स (sa) – he; कथं (kathaṃ) – how; न (na) – not; भवेत् (bhavet) – becomes; तव (tava) – your; पदमेव (padameva) – feet indeed (pada:feet | eva:indeed); परम्पदमिति (parampadamiti) – highest goal (param:highest | padam:goal | iti:thus); अनुशीलयतः (anuśīlayataḥ) – earnestly believing; मम (mama) – my; किं (kiṃ) – what; न (na) – not; शिवे (śive) – O auspicious goddess; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
कनकल सत्कलसिन्धुजलैरनुषिञ्चति तेगुणरङ्गभुवम्
भजति स किं न शचीकुचकुम्भ तटीपरिरम्भसुखानुभवम् ।
तव चरणं शरणं करवाणि नतामरवाणि निवासि शिवम्
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Kanaka lasatkala sindhujalairanuṣiñ cati teguṇaraṅgabhuvam
Bhajati sa kiṃ na śacīkucakumbha taṭīparirambh asukhānubhavam .
Tava caraṇaṃ śaraṇaṃ karavāṇi natāmaravāṇi nivāsi śivam
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 19 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
19. Mother Durga – An Abode of Devi Mahasaraswati
If someone bathes you, the playground of virtues, with shining golden waters of the ocean . will he not experience in heaven the happiness (equal to that of Indra) embracing the full bosom of Sachi? . O Goddess worshipped by the speech of Gods, I take refuge in your feet, which are also the abode of Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: कनकल (kanakala) – golden; सत्कल (satkala) – shining; सिन्धुजलैः (sindhujalaiḥ) – with ocean waters (sindhu:ocean | jalaiḥ:with waters); अनुषिञ्चति (anuṣiñcati) – bathes; ते (te) – your; गुणरङ्गभुवम् (guṇaraṅgabhuvam) – playground of virtues (guṇa:virtues | raṅgabhuvam:playground); भजति (bhajati) – experiences; स (sa) – he; किं (kiṃ) – not; न (na) – not; शचीकुचकुम्भ (śacīkucakumbha) – bosom of Sachi (śacī:Sachi | kucakumbha:bosom); तटीपरिरम्भ (taṭīparirambha) – embracing (taṭī:bosom | parirambha:embracing); सुखानुभवम् (sukhānubhavam) – happiness experience (sukha:happiness | anubhavam:experience); तव (tava) – your; चरणं (caraṇaṃ) – feet; शरणं (śaraṇaṃ) – refuge; करवाणि (karavāṇi) – I take; नतामरवाणि (natāmaravāṇi) – speech of Gods (nata:bowed | amaravāṇi:speech of Gods); निवासि (nivāsi) – abode; शिवम् (śivam) – auspicious; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – O; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – slayer of Mahishasura (mahiṣāsura:Mahishasura | mardini:slayer); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – beautifully braided (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:braided); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
तव विमलेन्दुकुलं वदनेन्दुमलं सकलं ननु कूलयते
किमु पुरुहूतपुरीन्दु मुखी सुमुखीभिरसौ विमुखीक्रियते ।
मम तु मतं शिवनामधने भवती कृपया किमुत क्रियते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Tava vimalendukulaṃ vadanendumalaṃ sakalaṃ nanu kūlayate
Kimu puruhūtapurīndu mukhī sumukhībhirasau vimukhīkriyate .
Mama tu mataṃ śivanāmadhane bhavatī kṛpayā kimuta kriyate
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 20 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
20. Mother Durga – Whose Pure Moon-Like Face Subdues our Impurities
Oh Pure One, the person who looks upon your moonlike face together with its digits causing a bond (with your essence) as the bank (beyond which the river of sorrow does not proceed) - will he ever be turned away by the ladies of Indra's City, having moonlike faces and auspicious faces ? Oh Goddess, having the name of Siva as her treasure, or my opinion is that with kindness you achieve it. Oh Goddess, who has crushed the demon, Mahisha, wearing a beatiful know of hair, Oh Daughter of Mountain (Himalaya), Victory to you, Victory to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; विमलेन्दुकुलं (vimalendukulaṃ) – pure moon-like face (vimala:pure | indu:moon | kulam:face); वदनेन्दुमलं (vadanendumalaṃ) – moon-like face (vadana:face | indu:moon | malam:pure); सकलं (sakalaṃ) – entire; ननु (nanu) – indeed; कूलयते (kūlayate) – subdues; किमु (kimu) – what; पुरुहूतपुरीन्दु (puruhūtapurīndu) – Indra's city moon-like (puru:Indra | hūta:called | purī:city | indu:moon); मुखी (mukhī) – faced; सुमुखीभिः (sumukhībhiḥ) – with auspicious faces (su:auspicious | mukhī:faced); असौ (asau) – he; विमुखीक्रियते (vimukhīkriyate) – turned away; मम (mama) – my; तु (tu) – but; मतं (mataṃ) – opinion; शिवनामधने (śivanāmadhane) – name of Siva as treasure (śiva:Siva | nāma:name | dhane:as treasure); भवती (bhavatī) – you; कृपया (kṛpayā) – with kindness; किमुत (kimuta) – or; क्रियते (kriyate) – achieve; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; हे (he) – oh; महिषासुरमर्दिनि (mahiṣāsuramardini) – crusher of Mahisha demon (mahiṣa:Mahisha | asura:demon | mardini:crusher); रम्यकपर्दिनि (ramyakapardini) – wearing beautiful hair knot (ramya:beautiful | kapardini:hair knot); शैलसुते (śailasute) – daughter of the mountain (śaila:mountain | sute:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
अयि मयि दीनदयालुतया कृपयैव त्वया भवितव्यमुमे
अयि जगतो जननी कृपयासि यथासि तथाऽनुमितासि रते ।
यदुचितमत्र भवत्युररीकुरुतादुरुतापमपाकुरुते
जय जय हे महिषासुरमर्दिनि रम्यकपर्दिनि शैलसुते ॥ २१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Ayi mayi dīna dayālutayā kṛpayaiva tvayā bhavitavyamume
Ayi jagato jananī kṛpayāsi yathāsi tathānumitāsirate .
Yaducitamatra bhavatyurarīkurutādurutāpamapākurute
Jaya jaya he mahiṣāsuramardini ramyakapardini śailasute .. 21 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
21. Mother Durga – Who Shower Grace on Devotees in the same manner as Arrows on Enemies
O Uma, you should be kindly disposed toward me because of your virtue of compassion toward the meek . (meaning unclear) . you may choose to do (with me) whatever is appropriate, she removes the great pain (of her devotees)
